review_title: On the path to becoming an HTB Hacker
description: I have been studying at Hack The Box for a year and a half since I started studying cybersecurity and it is very good for those who do not have much knowledge in the area
author: Maria Eduarda D.
pros: I like the Hack The Box platform because it allows me to study subjects that usually don't have enough material for online studies, and the practical teaching method is the best
cons: some modules are a bit complicated to understand and some are very extensive

author: Goh S.
pros: When it comes to anything cybersecurity, HackTheBox is my favorite training platform for learning and improving my skills. Their academy platform has a ton of great modules covering every topic of interest at an affordable and worth-it price. I am currently doing the penetration tester job role path and I can confidently say that I am able to learn new things in each one of them as they go so in depth in the topics. The best thing is that these modules are not purely just text, they are also accompanied by some challenging practices that force you to always think outside of the box, which is very important in this field. Besides academy, their main platform is also the perfect place for practicing cyber. Even for free users like myself, I am still able to play machines and challenges that are very important when it comes to upskilling and putting our knowledge to test. They have challenges that ranges from beginner to advanced level so that everyone with different skill level is able to upskill. I will 100% recommend anyone whether they are just starting or looking to up their cybersecurity skills to try out HackTheBox, it has truly helped me a ton in my career.
cons: I don't have any big complains about Hack The Box and I seriously mean this. It is the perfect platform for cybersecurity training and I couldn't thank it enough

review_title: Solid labs for pentesting, but not for red teams
author: Jeremy B.
pros: VPN network stability is top notch. Pro labs have a larger number of systems and servers which are more realistic than typical boot-to-root labs.
cons: Data mining makes the environment hostile to the use of custom tooling. One VPN session per user combined with no internet access for lab machines and no ACLs allowing traffic between VPN-connected users makes the environment unsuitable for red teams practicing use of C2 frameworks.

description: Hack The Box is a platform that became my 2nd home, like a Dojo: it has an uplifting community and the challenging content keeps me growing.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: + easy accessible, offers a lot of value even with free plan + different scalings (challenges, machines and ProLabs) + great community with a delight sense of humor + great support + the content is up-to-date (e.g. new CVE's in Dedicated Labs)
cons: The CPTS certification lacks something between the path and the exam (like Ippsec's unofficial CPTSPrep and/or a pro lab like Dante).

description: Overall good, the platform is a lot more mature then it was a few years ago and feels like its going in a positive direction.
author: Joseph G.
pros: The variety of content. There is so much different content in different subjects that allow someone to learn a lot of different content.
cons: The start up time for boxes and academy modules. Depending on the academy module it feels like im spending more time spinning up than actually doing the module.

review_title: A Challenging and Rewarding Journey into Cybersecurity
description: My overall experience with Hack The Box has been highly rewarding and educational. The platform offers a diverse range of machines and challenges that simulate real-world scenarios, which helped me strengthen my penetration testing and problem-solving skills. I appreciated the hands-on learning approach and the active community that shares insights and techniques. The gamified environment kept me motivated, and progressing through ranks gave a clear sense of achievement. While some challenges were difficult, they pushed me to learn and research deeper. Overall, Hack The Box has been a valuable tool in my cybersecurity journey and an excellent supplement to formal learning.
author: Sokheng K.
pros: Their training and course help me gain my knowledge and help me confident on my cyber security jobs.
cons: One of the things I liked least about Hack The Box is the inconsistent difficulty curve across some of the machines.

description: Got cyber security certified. Landed a part time position in offensive cyber tactics, with one of the worlds leading cyber security teams. Thanks to Hack The Box.
author: Joshua B.
pros: The available list of choices. like real life scenarios, industry certifications, Hands on upskilling. To cybersecurity fundamentals.
cons: The tasks are very challenging. This is to be expected. Its just the technique used to train in your chosen field is a bit of a un-human like experience. Its quite confusing at times.

description: The Pwnbox is super handy and deploys very quickly! The progress bar on each page on academy helps to keep me reading and the overall progress on job role path is nice to see too. The formatting and arrangement of lessons and subsections within lessons has really been thought about, you can tell it's not just random bits of knowledge put together but a more comprehensive piece. I like that the overall user experience and user learning experience has been thought through, this makes a big difference compared to other platform where subjects are just collated together, on Hack The Box there is a real sense of a learning journey.
author: Mathieu ..
pros: I love the platform, the integration between theory & practice. I use it the platform almost everyday and can only recommend it. I contacted the customer support of couple of times and it has been very smooth, quick answer and resolution. Hack The Box is so much more than the platform, the community is amazing - weekly cube talks, discord and regular online events... I learn new things everyday and can see a direct application to my work. I find that in the overwhelming amount of online courses it's easy to get lost but HTB Academy has been the reference for me. Once I get the theory I can apply it straightaway to a box or a small exercise to reinforce that knowledge. I only hope that in a couple of years the certifications become the new industry standards, widely accepted by recruiters/HR and technical teams.
cons: I wish the daily streak feature was implemented on the HTB Enterprise platform as well as the ability for certifications gained through the Enterprise subscription to sync to the HTB Account. Ideally anything that is gained and completed should show and be accessible on the user's account outside of the Enterprise platform. I would say there are still a few typos here and there in some Academy pages and it would be nice to have a simple way to report them so that the community can help the product improve. Some of the pages are really long so a way to break this up would be great but it's always a tight balance between a long page to read vs a lot of short pages to read, overall it's pretty good. One last thing, if HTB certifications could offer equivalency with industry standards like CREST CRT or The Cyber Scheme CSTM (like the OSCP equivalency with CREST) that would be even better!

review_title: HTB, I love it so much.
author: Souldream S.
pros: The material is so comperhensive, robust, clear and easy to understand. And I hope the material is always up to date.
cons: In the very rare moment the lab have a little glitch. I just follow the instruction but still nothing.

author: Abdullah M.
pros: Comprehensive and extra detailed knowledge. It is so guided and informative. Focusing on making professional cybersecurity specialists
cons: It might not be for all levels , specially the begginers, but they made a specific role path for beginners
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: I've come to appreciate the pace at which I study, and I'm proud to achieve the goals set by Hack The Box. The platform's material has helped me explore new areas while strengthening my knowledge. I also love the flexibility of picking up where I left off, even after taking a week or two off, allowing me to review and continue progressing seamlessly. The material has made it so easy to learn even when the subject is extremely complex and difficult to convey. The team really made a great platform
author: Matthew W.
pros: Hack The Box is an excellent platform for beginners looking to enhance their cybersecurity skills and for seasoned professionals wanting to stay sharp. Its standout feature is flexibility, allowing you to learn at your own pace. If you get stuck, the platform has a helpful forum that guides you without simply giving away the answers. Not only does Hack The Box provide a comprehensive set of learning resources, but it also allows you to apply what you've learned through practical exercises. The regularly updated environments offer new and exciting challenges, ensuring there's always something fresh to hack.
cons: There is nothing that I feel I can put into this section.

author: Jaden L.
pros: I can learn a lot through this platform.
cons: Maybe the arrangement of the modules, sometimes it's hidden and can be hard for new users to find past work.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Pierre-Alexandre D.
pros: Really like how it is build and how convenient the learning process feels in whatever path you choose.
cons: The fact that the student subscription is limited to Tier 2 with no reduction or annual membership that could be worth it for higher tier access.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: D'une manière globale je suis satisfait par HTB, par contre la hausse de tarif est assez soudaine! c'est un peu dommage de devoir repayer et se recréer un nouveau compte pour accéder à HTB Academy par exemple. A l'avenir il faudrait trouver un moyen de pouvoir transféré des point ou de l'expérience d'un compte perso vers un compte pro et vice versa car j'ai commencé HTB avec un compte perso puis mon entreprise m'a créé un compte pro mais je ne peux pas capitaliser d'un compte vers l'autre.
author: willy G.
pros: Le contenu technique est pertinent et très pointu. Rien n'est laissé au hasard, même sur les sujets connu et répandu il y a toujours un approfondissement du sujet traité au travers d'une vrais recherche didactique. On sent bien que les personnes qui ont fait le contenu ont pris soin de bien vérifier et validé les résultats. Cela reflète bien le niveau technique des experts qui travaille chez HTB, bravo !
cons: Je pense qu'il faudrait donner la possibilité de pouvoir télécharger d'une manière ou d'une autre le contenu des cours de manière à avoir un pense bête ou un memo au format PDF par exemple. Même si je comprends bien que le contenu est dynamique et doit être mis à jour assez régulièrement.

author: Malik M.
pros: The courses are up to date and the labs work like expected.
cons: The prices for tier 3 and 4 modules are expensive.

review_title: A Honest Review form an undergraduate
description: Overall it is a best place to build.ur experience and get ready for the OSCP exam.
author: Udumala N.
pros: I love the content or study material in terms of academy and Normal hack the box. I love the retired rooms feature which help me in starting the HTB. I also love the University CTF which are being conducted.
cons: One of the big thing I have is individual domain for each thing like ctf, academy which they r joining now. It is hard for beginners who are starting with cybersecurity.

description: Hack The Box Academy is an amazing but challenging place to learn. The labs included in the modules are awesome!
author: Gonzalo P.
pros: The labs are complex and allow you to apply what you have just learned in the theoretical part.
cons: The duration of the Labs is for a limited time. Probably to save resources but sometimes when you are in the middle of a practice you run out of time and you have to launch the lab again.

review_title: Learn or improve your hacking skills
author: Michal O.
pros: Hack the Box has very quality content. I learned a lot from this platform. It contains a lot gamified content. Also it is not too expensive and it worth the money.
cons: Sometimes infrastructure is not 100% working, and the web contains several less severe bugs. I had several issues with the bugs in the web and infrastructure. All my issue was solved with HTB support.

review_title: Great place to learn and experiment
author: Mahad A.
pros: Everyone at different skill levels can use it. If you're brand new to computers, you can learn from HTB Academy and improve your skills. pwnboxes in academy will let you experiment and implement what you've learned right on the spot. HTB has different ranks which you can use to show off to potential employers as well.
cons: It gets difficult at times, and any small mistakes in the answers by the author can result in hours of wasted time trying to figure out the "solution"

author: Srinivas A.
pros: Structured way of learning Its ease of use Customer Support User Interface
cons: Too technical words in Theory part and difficulty in navigating through different hackthebox sites

description: I have been using HTB for 5+ years and has been an amazing journey that helped me expand my CyberSecurity knowldege.
author: Alessandro M.
pros: HTB is a FREE platform that allows ANYONE to get acquaintance of Cyber Security world up to expert level.
cons: The ranking system makes it hard to level up.

author: Alvaro P.
pros: Tons of courses and labs. It makes It easy to learn dificult concepts.
cons: None.

author: Roberto A.
pros: Ease of use, realistic scenarios, different rates of challenges. The gamified approach keeps the users engaged while fostering collaboration and learning.
cons: Some boxes can be quite challenging, especially for beginners.

review_title: Perfect for learning in Real Labs
author: Avyash R.
pros: The pro labs are the best. Its like the real patched machines which will challenge you about your skills.
cons: Everything is hood except that it could get little cheaper for someone like students or beginners who wants to start their cyber journey.

review_title: Pentester job Path - prepping for CPTS
description: I’m currently on the Pen Tester Path at Hack The Box, prepping for the CPTS certification, and I’m beyond thrilled with the experience—I’m absolutely blown away! HTB delivers an outstanding mix of in-depth lessons and hands-on practice that truly sets it apart. Both their Academy and Labs are packed with top-notch, practical content that dives deep into real-world scenarios. Before HTB, I was on TryHackMe, where I reached the top 1% of users, but I found it focused mainly on basic to medium-level challenges with less emphasis on hands-on learning. HTB takes things to a whole new level with its structured, real-world approach. I’m incredibly satisfied with the platform, and the subscription is absolutely worth every penny for anyone serious about advancing their cybersecurity skills. Huge props to the HTB team for building such an exceptional platform!
author: Nikola P.
pros: What I love most about Hack The Box is the hands-on approach and the way it dives into essential details. It’s not just a collection of random knowledge—there’s a clear structure that guides you toward your goal. Absolutely perfect.
cons: I think Hack The Box can sometimes feel a bit overwhelming for beginners, as it occasionally assumes prior knowledge of certain concepts or terminology, which might be confusing if you’re just starting out.
